results.question: What is the name of the framework proposed for evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation pipelines?

results.question: In the Ragas paper, what type of language models can be used with retrieval-augmented strategies?
results.generated_answer: According to the Ragas paper, standard LLMs can be used with retrieval-augmented strategies, in addition to specialized LMs [1, N/A]. results.question: What retrieval algorithm is outperformed by Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR)?
results.generated_answer: BM25 is outperformed by Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR) [1, Abstract].

results.question: What type of encoder architecture is utilized in Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR)?
results.generated_answer: Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR) utilizes a dual-encoder architecture [1, 3.1], [2, Introduction], [3, 2 Related Work].

results.question: In the paper on improving dense passage retrieval, what is the effect of associating multiple positive passages with each question during training?
results.generated_answer: The effect of associating multiple positive passages with each question during training is that it consistently improves retrieval accuracy, even when using a significantly smaller batch size [1, Introduction], [3, 2.2]. This is because it provides the model with more positive feedback during training, reformulating the training process as a binary classification task [3, 2.2].

results.question: In the negative-aware training (NAT) paper on learning from failure, what type of models are used as the core of the agent system?
results.generated_answer: In the negative-aware training (NAT) paper on learning from failure, Large Language Models (LLMs) are used as the core of the agent system [1, 3].

results.question: Which LLM is used to generate trajectories in the negative-aware training (NAT) paper on learning from failure?
results.generated_answer: The LLM used to generate trajectories is GPT-3.5/4 [2, 2.1 Fine-tuning LLMs as Agents].

results.question: In the negative-aware training (NAT) paper, what is the proposed method to improve model performance using negative examples?
results.generated_answer: The proposed method, Negative-Aware Training (NAT), involves incorporating negative examples into the fine-tuning process to improve model performance [1, N/A]. This is achieved by appending a specific phrase to the negative examples, such as "Please generate a solution that incorrectly answers the question" [1, N/A]. The NAT method is designed to provide a better trade-off between useful information and errors in negative examples [3, N/A].

results.question: In the retrieval oriented masking (ROM) paper, what percentage of masked tokens are stop-words or punctuation?
results.generated_answer: According to the paper, about 40% of the masked tokens produced by the 15% random masking method are stop-words or punctuation [1, 1 Introduction], [3, 3.2 Disadvantages of Random Masking], [4, Abstract].

results.question: What is the name of the proposed token masking method for dense passage retrieval pretraining?
results.generated_answer: The proposed token masking method for dense passage retrieval pretraining is called Retrieval Oriented Masking (ROM) [1, 1 Introduction], [2, N/A], [4, 3.3 Retrieval Oriented Masking].

results.question: In the retrieval oriented masking (ROM) paper, what is the proportion of random masking used?
results.generated_answer: The proportion of random masking used is 15% [1, 1 Introduction], [2, N/A].
